response:
To determine whether \(22717\) is prime, test divisibility by all primes up to

\[
\sqrt{22717} \approx 150.7
\]

So we only need to check prime divisors up to \(149\).

## Step 1: Basic checks
- \(22717\) is odd, so not divisible by \(2\).
- Sum of digits: \(2+2+7+1+7=19\), so not divisible by \(3\).
- Does not end in \(0\) or \(5\), so not divisible by \(5\).

## Step 2: Test small primes
Check successive primes:

- \(7:\; 22717 \div 7 = 3245\) remainder \(2\)
- \(11:\; 22717 \div 11 = 2065\) remainder \(2\)
- \(13:\; 13 \cdot 1747 = 22711\), remainder \(6\)
- \(17:\; 17 \cdot 1336 = 22712\), remainder \(5\)
- \(19:\; 19 \cdot 1195 = 22705\), remainder \(12\)
- \(23:\; 23 \cdot 987 = 22701\), remainder \(16\)
- \(29:\; 29 \cdot 783 = 22707\), remainder \(10\)
- \(31:\; 31 \cdot 733 = 22723\), not divisible
- \(37:\; 37 \cdot 614 = 22718\), not divisible

Continue:

- \(41:\; 41 \cdot 554 = 22714\), remainder \(3\)
- \(43:\; 43 \cdot 528 = 22704\), remainder \(13\)
- \(47:\; 47 \cdot 483 = 22701\), remainder \(16\)
- \(53:\; 53 \cdot 428 = 22684\), remainder \(33\)
- \(59:\; 59 \cdot 385 = 22715\), remainder \(2\)
- \(61:\; 61 \cdot 372 = 22692\), remainder \(25\)
- \(67:\; 67 \cdot 339 = 22713\), remainder \(4\)
- \(71:\; 71 \cdot 320 = 22720\), not divisible
- \(73:\; 73 \cdot 311 = 22703\), remainder \(14\)
- \(79:\; 79 \cdot 287 = 22673\), remainder \(44\)
- \(83:\; 83 \cdot 273 = 22659\), remainder \(58\)
- \(89:\; 89 \cdot 255 = 22695\), remainder \(22\)
- \(97:\; 97 \cdot 234 = 22698\), remainder \(19\)

Continue further:

- \(101:\; 101 \cdot 225 = 22725\), not divisible
- \(103:\; 103 \cdot 220 = 22660\), remainder \(57\)
- \(107:\; 107 \cdot 212 = 22684\), remainder \(33\)
- \(109:\; 109 \cdot 208 = 22672\), remainder \(45\)
- \(113:\; 113 \cdot 201 = 22713\), remainder \(4\)
- \(127:\; 127 \cdot 178 = 22606\), remainder \(111\)
- \(131:\; 131 \cdot 173 = 22663\), remainder \(54\)
- \(137:\; 137 \cdot 166 = 22742\), not divisible
- \(139:\; 139 \cdot 163 = 22657\), remainder \(60\)
- \(149:\; 149 \cdot 152 = 22648\), remainder \(69\)

None of the primes up to \(149\) divide \(22717\).

## Conclusion
Since \(22717\) has no prime divisor less than or equal to \(\sqrt{22717}\), it is **prime**.
